key_insight: OmicFormer is a Transformer that builds in statistical priors about feature-label and feature-feature relationships to make omics-based disease and trait prediction more accurate and more robust across cohorts.
clinical_relevance: By improving generalization under distribution shifts, this approach could support more dependable biomarker-based risk prediction and precision medicine applications across diverse populations.
method: Uses a Transformer backbone for high-dimensional omics representation learning
Injects two priors: feature-label associations and feature-feature dependencies
Captures both local and long-range biological interactions that standard models may miss
evidence_signal: The model was evaluated on 500,000 UK Biobank participants across 450 disease and 900 trait prediction tasks, and it also generalized well in independent proteomics and multi-site neuroimaging cohorts.
watch_item: Performance gains are shown in retrospective benchmarking, so prospective validation and careful handling of cohort bias are still needed before real-world deployment.
paper_title: OmicFormer: a statistical priors-informed transformer for accurate and generalizable omics prediction of diseases and complex traits
